content.introduction: Radix sort orders keys by processing digits from least to most significant (or vice versa) using stable sub-sorts.

sections.title: 7.8.1 Key Ideas and Model
sections.content: Define digit decomposition, radix/base, and stability requirement of per-digit sorting.
examples: Explain why stability is mandatory for radix correctness

sections.title: 7.8.2 Representation and Workflow
sections.content: Describe LSD workflow with counting/bucket distribution and collection per digit position.
examples: Run counting distribution at each digit

sections.title: 7.8.3 Complexity and Practical Guidance
sections.content: Complexity is O(d(n + k)) for d digits and base size k; discuss constraints on key formats.
examples: Compare radix with comparison-based lower bounds
Use radix for fixed-length integer/string keys
Evaluate memory usage of bucket arrays

keyTakeaways: Apply radix sort to suitable key domains
